projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The persistent rapid toggle between 20 and 21 connected devices, combined with zero camera activity and the early Friday morning timeframe, strongly indicates a single indoor device is intermittently connecting and disconnecting rather than reflecting actual household occupancy. This confirms the previously flagged sensor drift and suggests it is likely triggering unnecessary automations or network polling.

The presence sensor reports 19-20 mobile devices home with rapid 19/20 fluctuations at 6 AM, which is unusually high for a four-person household and suggests either unexpected guests, a network polling glitch, or unknown devices on the Wi-Fi. Verification is recommended to rule out security or connectivity issues.

The sustained 18-20 device count with zero camera motion at 11:27 PM confirms the household is asleep in non-monitored rooms, but the unusually high count likely stems from Wi-Fi presence detection artifacts or background IoT polling rather than actual occupancy; no security concern, but worth verifying network settings if it persists.

The steady increase in mobile device count (19 to 21) paired with zero camera motion over four hours suggests either guests are gathered in a camera-blind zone or the presence system is miscounting stationary IoT devices. This warrants a quick verification to ensure normal evening activity and confirm camera functionality.

The sustained 17-21 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours indicates the household is congregated in a camera-blind area (likely a media room or basement) in a low-activity state, consistent with a typical Thursday evening wind-down.

The sustained 15-21 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours indicates the household is likely hosting guests or gathering in a camera-blind zone for a stationary activity like a movie night. This matters because it confirms a social gathering while highlighting a potential blind spot that should be verified to ensure cameras are functioning as expected.

The sustained 14-16 device count paired with zero camera motion over the last 2.5 hours confirms the household is gathered in a single, motion-blind area (likely the living room) for a stationary activity like a movie, game night, or dinner with guests. This cross-signal pattern aligns with the earlier flags and indicates normal evening congregation rather than a system anomaly.

The sustained 13-16 device count with zero camera motion indicates the household is likely congregated in a camera-blind zone or has motion sensitivity set too low, which masks routine activity and suggests a shift to a stationary gathering or a system configuration gap.

The sustained 13-16 device count paired with zero camera motion strongly indicates stationary presence (e.g., devices left charging, remote work/study sessions, or guests in an unmonitored room) rather than active household movement, explaining the lack of environmental triggers despite the high presence count.

The sustained 14-17 device count combined with zero camera motion or environmental activity for four hours on a weekday afternoon strongly suggests a sensor or camera malfunction rather than normal household inactivity. A quick system diagnostic is recommended to verify sensor integrity.
